page.title: Yacht Charter Puerto Banús 2026: 17-Boat Fleet From €749

data.meta_description: Book a yacht charter Puerto Banús 2026 from €749/2h. 17-boat fleet includes Mangusta, Azimut & Ferretti. Skipper, drinks & VAT included. Reserve now.
data.summary: Discover the 17-boat fleet for yacht charter Puerto Banús, with prices from €749/2h. All charters include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and 21% VAT—no hidden extras.
key_facts: All charters depart from Puerto Banús, Marbella’s superyacht marina.
Every booking includes licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, wine, cava, and light snacks.
Spanish IVA (21%) included in all quoted prices – no hidden fees.
Tier B boats (Mangusta 80, white & grey) include a free jet ski for the charter duration.
Other boats (Ferretti 94, Canados 86, Pershing 46) – pricing on request via WhatsApp.
Suitable for groups of 5 to 12 passengers depending on the boat.
Day trips can reach Cabopino, Estepona, Sotogrande, or Cala del Faro.

faq.q: What is included in the yacht charter Puerto Banús price?
faq.a: All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no extra charges for these items. The price you see is the price you pay.

faq.q: How much does a yacht charter in Puerto Banús cost?
faq.a: Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (Tier A). For larger vessels like the Mangusta 80, a minimum 4-hour charter costs €4,719 and includes a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Which boats are available for charter in Puerto Banús?
faq.a: We operate 17 boats from Puerto Banús. The fleet includes Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80 (white and grey), Azimut 58 Flybridge, Bandido, Canados 86, Dubhe, Fairline Targa 12m, Ferretti 94, K80, Lagoon 380, Mariah SX21, Pershing 46, Red Tide, and a 7m speedboat.

faq.q: Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. We already provide water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There is no corkage fee. Just let your skipper know in advance.

faq.q: Do I need a boating license to charter a yacht in Puerto Banús?
faq.a: No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles navigation, docking, and safety. You and your guests can relax and enjoy the experience without any licence or prior experience.

faq.q: What are the departure and return points for the charter?
faq.a: All charters depart from and return to Puerto Banús, Marbella. The marina is located on the Golden Mile, with easy parking and taxi access. Your skipper will confirm the exact berth before departure.

faq.q: Can we swim or stop for a swim during the charter?
faq.a: Yes, provided weather and sea conditions allow. Popular swimming spots include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coves near Cabopino. Your skipper will choose the safest and most scenic anchorage.

faq.q: How do I book a yacht charter in Puerto Banús?
faq.a: You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for personalised advice and pricing on larger boats. A deposit secures your date, and the balance is due before departure.

data.body_html:
Puerto Banús is the undisputed centre of Marbella’s yachting scene, and a yacht charter Puerto Banús in 2026 gives you access to a fleet of 17 vessels ranging from €749 for two hours to €4,719 for a four-hour superyacht experience. Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and Spanish VAT at 21% – no hidden extras. Whether you want a fast RIB for a sunset spin or a 29-metre Ferretti for a day cruise to Estepona, the boats are ready in the marina.

Why Choose a Yacht Charter in Puerto Banús?

Puerto Banús is not just a marina; it’s a landmark. Located on Marbella’s Golden Mile, it hosts some of the Mediterranean’s finest superyachts and offers direct access to the Costa del Sol’s best cruising grounds. From here you can reach Cabopino in 20 minutes, Estepona in 40 minutes, or Sotogrande in an hour. The water temperature in summer averages 24°C, and the prevailing westerly winds make for comfortable sailing or motoring. With 17 boats in our fleet, you can match the vessel exactly to your group size and budget.

Our Fleet: 17 Boats from €749

We operate the largest dedicated charter fleet in Puerto Banús. Here is the full list:

  • Astondoa 40 – 12.5m, 9 pax, from €749/2h
  • Azimut 39 – 12.5m, 11 pax, from €749/2h
  • Mangusta 80 (white & grey) – 24m, 12 pax, €4,719/4h (jet ski included)
  • Azimut 58 Flybridge – 17.6m, 12 pax, pricing on request
  • Bandido – 11.5m, 8 pax, pricing on request
  • Canados 86 – 26m, 12 pax, pricing on request
  • Dubhe – 8m, 5 pax, pricing on request
  • Fairline Targa 12m – 12m, 10 pax, pricing on request
  • Ferretti 94 – 29m, 12 pax, pricing on request
  • K80 – 24m, 12 pax, pricing on request
  • Lagoon 380 – 11.6m, 10 pax, pricing on request
  • Mariah SX21 – 6.4m, 5 pax, pricing on request
  • Pershing 46 – 14m, 10 pax, pricing on request
  • Red Tide – 11m, 6 pax, pricing on request
  • Speedboat (7m) – 7m, 6 pax, pricing on request

For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) the price is fixed at €749 for 2 hours or €2,299 for a full 8-hour day. All other boats are priced individually – contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

What’s Included in Every Charter

Every booking includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no extra charges for crew, fuel, or taxes. You can bring your own food and additional drinks without corkage. The skipper will handle navigation, mooring, and safety – you just relax.

Popular Destinations from Puerto Banús

Your skipper will tailor the route to your preferences. Common options include:

  • Cabo Pino – a quiet beach cove 15 minutes east, with clear water for swimming.
  • Cala del Faro – a rocky inlet with a lighthouse, 20 minutes west.
  • Estepona – a charming fishing town with a long promenade, 40 minutes west.
  • Sotogrande – a luxury marina and golf resort, 1 hour west.
  • Marbella Marina – a quick stop for lunch or a drink, 10 minutes east.

Your skipper will advise on the best route based on weather and sea conditions. Most charters include at least one swimming stop.

How to Book Your Yacht Charter Puerto Banús

Booking is straightforward. Browse our fleet on the Boat Rental Marbella homepage, select your preferred boat and date, and contact us via WhatsApp for availability. For Tier A boats you can book directly online; for all others we will provide a personalised quote within minutes. A deposit secures your booking, with the balance due before departure. We recommend booking at least 48 hours in advance, especially during July and August.

Tips for a Great Day on the Water

  • Arrive 15 minutes early at the marina – your skipper will meet you at the berth.
  • Bring sunscreen, sunglasses, a hat, and a light jacket – even in summer the sea breeze can be cool.
  • If you want to swim, bring a towel and swimwear. The skipper will find a calm spot.
  • Let us know if you have any dietary requirements – we can adjust the snacks and drinks.
  • For larger groups, consider the Mangusta 80 which includes a free jet ski – a huge hit with guests.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is included in the yacht charter Puerto Banús price?

All our charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). There are no extra charges for these items. The price you see is the price you pay.

How much does a yacht charter in Puerto Banús cost?

Prices start at €749 for 2 hours on an Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39 (Tier A). For larger vessels like the Mangusta 80, a minimum 4-hour charter costs €4,719 and includes a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Which boats are available for charter in Puerto Banús?

We operate 17 boats from Puerto Banús. The fleet includes Astondoa 40, Azimut 39, Mangusta 80 (white and grey), Azimut 58 Flybridge, Bandido, Canados 86, Dubhe, Fairline Targa 12m, Ferretti 94, K80, Lagoon 380, Mariah SX21, Pershing 46, Red Tide, and a 7m speedboat.

Can I bring my own food and drinks on board?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. We already provide water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, and light snacks. There is no corkage fee. Just let your skipper know in advance.

Do I need a boating license to charter a yacht in Puerto Banús?

No. Every charter includes a licensed skipper who handles navigation, docking, and safety. You and your guests can relax and enjoy the experience without any licence or prior experience.

What are the departure and return points for the charter?

All charters depart from and return to Puerto Banús, Marbella. The marina is located on the Golden Mile, with easy parking and taxi access. Your skipper will confirm the exact berth before departure.

Can we swim or stop for a swim during the charter?

Yes, provided weather and sea conditions allow. Popular swimming spots include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coves near Cabopino. Your skipper will choose the safest and most scenic anchorage.

How do I book a yacht charter in Puerto Banús?

You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for personalised advice and pricing on larger boats. A deposit secures your date, and the balance is due before departure.
